scenario_title: add_rr to a name that currently owns a CNAME

group_1.behavior: Reject the UPDATE because the target name already owns a CNAME, leaving the zone unchanged.
group_1.post_state: alias.campus.edu. remains a CNAME to target.campus.edu.; no A RRset is present at alias.campus.edu.

group_2.behavior: Accept the UPDATE with NOERROR but normalize the zone by preserving the CNAME and ignoring the new A, or otherwise make the result appear unchanged on follow-up queries.
group_2.post_state: alias.campus.edu. remains a CNAME to target.campus.edu.; no A RRset is present at alias.campus.edu.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §10.1 states that a CNAME owner must not have other data, but RFC 2136 does not specify a required UPDATE error or normalization rule when an add_rr violates that invariant. As a result, implementations may reject the UPDATE (e.g. YXRRSET) or accept it while preserving the CNAME and ignoring the new A RR, making both reply code and resulting state underspecified.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: This scenario creates a live RFC 2136 UPDATE ambiguity by adding an A record directly to a name that already owns a CNAME. The zone is otherwise minimal and valid, and the follow-up queries to the CNAME and A RRsets at the touched name make it possible to observe whether the server rejected the update or accepted it while leaving the CNAME-only state intact.
